heading: Authorization of imposition of sanctions
label: 1741d Authorization of imposition of sanctions

children.text: is responsible for or is complicit in, or responsible for ordering, controlling, or otherwise directing, the hostage-taking of a United States national abroad or the unlawful or wrongful detention of a United States national abroad; or
knowingly provides financial, material, or technological support for, or goods or services in support of, an activity described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
children.label: 1 Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
i
children.text: inadmissible to the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: ineligible to receive a visa or other documentation to enter the United States; and

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise ineligible to be admitted or paroled into the United States or to receive any other benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et seq.).8 U.S.C. 1101

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An alien described in subsection (a) may be subject to revocation of any visa or other entry documentation regardless of when the visa or other entry documentation is or was issued.

children.label: I
children.text: take effect immediately; and

children.label: II
children.text: cancel any other valid visa or entry documentation that is in the alien’s possession.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The President may exercise all of the powers granted to the President under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.), to the extent necessary to block and prohibit all transactions in property and interests in property of a foreign person described in subsection (a) if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Inapplicability of national emergency requirement
children.label: B Inapplicability of national emergency requirement
children.text: The requirements of section 202 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act () shall not apply for purposes of this section.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Exception for intelligence activities
children.label: 1 Exception for intelligence activities
children.text: Sanctions under this section shall not apply to any activity subject to the reporting requirements under title V of the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.) or any authorized intelligence activities of the United States.50 U.S.C. 3091

children.heading: Exception to comply with international obligations and for law enforcement activities
children.label: 2 Exception to comply with international obligations and for law enforcement activities
A
children.text: to permit the United States to comply with the Agreement regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , between the United Nations and the United States, or other applicable international obligations; orJune 26, 1947November 21, 1947

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out or assist law enforcement activity in the United States.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: d Penalties
children.text: A person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of subsection (b)(2) or any regulation, license, or order issued to carry out that subsection shall be subject to the penalties set forth in subsections (b) and (c) of section 206 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act () to the same extent as a person that commits an unlawful act described in subsection (a) of that section.50 U.S.C. 1705
information exists that the person did not engage in the activity for which sanctions were imposed;
the person has been prosecuted appropriately for the activity for which sanctions were imposed;

the person has credibly demonstrated a significant change in behavior, has paid an appropriate consequence for the activity for which sanctions were imposed, and has credibly committed to not engage in an activity described in subsection (a) in the future; or
the termination of the sanctions is in the national security interests of the United States.

children.heading: Reporting requirement
children.label: f Reporting requirement
children.text: If the President terminates sanctions pursuant to subsection (d), the President shall report to the appropriate congressional committees a written justification for such termination within 15 days.1So in original. Probably should be “subsection (e),”.1

children.heading: Implementation of regulatory authority
children.label: g Implementation of regulatory authority
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1704) to carry out this section.50 U.S.C. 1702

children.heading: Exception relating to importation of goods
children.label: h Exception relating to importation of goods

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The authorities and requirements to impose sanctions authorized under this subchapter shall not include the authority or a requirement to impose sanctions on the importation of goods.

children.heading: Good defined
children.label: 2 Good defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “good” means any article, natural or manmade substance, material, supply or manufactured product, including inspection and test equipment, and excluding technical data.

children.label: A
children.text: any citizen or national of a foreign country (including any such individual who is also a citizen or national of the United States); or

children.label: B
children.text: any entity not organized solely under the laws of the United States or existing solely in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: an individual who is a United States citizen or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence to the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: an entity organized under the laws of the United States or any jurisdiction within the United States, including a foreign branch of such an entity; or

children.label: C
children.text: any person in the United States.
